Expense ratio 0.77% Benchmark Nifty Midcap 150 TR INR AUM ₹85358 Cr Inception Date 1 January, 2013 Min Lumpsum/SIP ₹100/₹100 Exit Load 1.0% Lock In No Lock-in TurnOver 18.22% Risk Very High Risk About HDFC Mid Cap Fund HDFC Mid Cap Opportunities Fund is a mid-cap mutual fund that is required by SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) to invest at least 65% of its assets in mid-sized companies . These are companies that rank 101st to 250th on the stock exchange as per their market capitalization. The fund uses the Nifty Mid Cap 150 TRI as its benchmark index. A benchmark index measures and compares the fund's performance against a broader market of mid-cap companies. HDFC Mid Cap Opportunities Fund maintains a strong focus on sectors like Financials, Automobiles, Healthcare, Technology, etc. Investment Strategy The fund manager adopts a bottom-up stock-picking approach, focusing on individual companies rather than sectors or market trends. The emphasis is on identifying business with: - Sound financial strength - Strong business model - Long-term growth potential Should I Invest In HDFC Mid Cap Opportunties Fund? HDFC Mid-cap fund invests in mid-sized companies. These companies are emerging businesses that are growing rapidly and hold the potential to become large-cap. If you wish to invest in the HDFC mid-cap fund, you must have the following traits: - Long-term investmentor - Moderate to high-risk appetite - Requires active management Taxation On HDFC Mid Cap Direct Growth Fund Tax liability across all equity mutual funds is the same. For HDFC Mid Cap Fund, you will be taxed on any profit you make on the sale of your investments. If you sell your investment within 12 months, it is termed as a Short-term capital gain (STCG). If you sell your investments after 12 months, it is termed as Long-term capital gain (LTCG). The taxes on your investment are the following: - Short-term Capital Gain Tax : 20% of STCG - Long-term Capital Gain Tax: LTCG Exempted up to 1,25,000 and then 12.5%. INDmoney makes it simple to invest in the HDFC Mid Cap Fund. To do so, follow the steps below: Download the INDmoney app. Search for ‘HDFC Mid Cap Fund’. Select whether you want to invest in SIP or lump sum. Enter the amount you wish to invest. Set up payments via bank mandate or UPI. What is the current NAV of HDFC Mid Cap Fund? The NAV of the fund today is ₹220.06. NAV or Net Asset Value is the per unit price of a mutual fund. For example, if the NAV of a fund is ₹10, it means you can get 1000 units of that mutual fund at ₹10,000. What is the return on the fund? HDFC Mid Cap Fund fund has generated a return of 14.96% in 1 year, 25.47% in 3 years, 22.95% in 5 years. Who is the fund manager of HDFC Mid Cap Fund? The fund managers are Dhruv Muchhal, Chirag Setalvad. What is the exit load of the fund? The exit load is 1% if redeemed in 0-1 Years. Exit load is a fee levied for ‘exiting’ the fund earlier than a stipulated period, usually 1 year. What is the expense ratio of the fund? The expense ratio is 0.77%. The Expense Ratio is a fee charged for managing the fund. This includes commissions, administrative costs etc. What is the AUM of the fund? The AUM of the fund is ₹85358 Cr. AUM stands for Assets Under Management. It refers to the total assets managed by the fund. What are the top holdings of HDFC Mid Cap Fund? The top 3 holdings of the fund are Max Financial Services Ltd(4.5%), The Federal Bank Ltd(3.88%), AU Small Finance Bank Ltd(3.88%) Invest Now
